reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome BEFORE building — the real ONNX graph repainted photos as anime, with a rendered-output screenshot reviewed; then the full demo was driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver across all 5 pages; desktop+mobile 0px overflow measured), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21, WASM, fresh profile) BEFORE building. The real AnimeGANv2 ONNX graphs (vumichien/AnimeGANv2_{Hayao,Shinkai,Paprika}) loaded via ort.InferenceSession.create. inputNames=['generator_input:0'] (NHWC), outputNames=['generator/G_MODEL/out_layer/Tanh:0']. Dynamic-size verified — 256², 384×256 and 512² all ran. On real photos each style produced a genuine anime repaint: normalized RMSE(out,in) 0.10-0.17, and a RENDERED-OUTPUT SCREENSHOT was reviewed (mountain landscape -> luminous Shinkai anime with smoothed painterly clouds, saturated sky, stylized foliage). NOT a colour filter (RMSE far above a curve), NOT identity, NOT degenerate (output std ~0.27-0.39). Then the deployed demo ran end-to-end: Anime-fy produced real output at inference 4019 ms @512 (restyle amount 0.078).
rubric.notes: Inference AND the tanh->RGBA composite run off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; the styled ImageBitmap is transferred (not cloned) back. The worker handles the TensorFlow NHWC channels-last layout + [-1,1] tanh range by hand.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = an anime repaint [1,H,W,3] (NHWC) in [-1,1], same spatial size as the (multiple-of-32-rounded) input. The worker maps (v+1)/2 back to RGB and clamps. Semantic sanity: the three styles diverge on the SAME input (Hayao gentle pastels, Shinkai saturated luminous skies, Paprika bold flat colour) and the restyle-amount metric tracks the change — evidence of a learned per-style repaint, not one filter with presets.
rubric.notes: The showcase's FIRST photo->art stylization demo. Distinct from every built restoration/enhancement/depth/segmentation demo. Built alongside a second, technically distinct stylizer (fast-neural-style-transfer).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model ids (vumichien/AnimeGANv2_{Hayao,Shinkai,Paprika}, linked), the task (image-to-image / cartoonization), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21 — noting transformers.js has no AnimeGAN class/task), the real I/O tensor names + shapes AND the unusual NHWC channels-last layout + [-1,1] tanh range (in at-a-glance + the How-the-API-works snippet), quantisation (fp32 single file per style), backend (WASM), download (~8.4 MB/style, lazy) and license (Apache-2.0). No invented API surface.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only (the isolated per-worker ORT-web escape hatch, like nafnet/scunet/uvdoc) — shared lib/webai.js is untouched. Styles load lazily so only picked styles download.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'vumichien/AnimeGANv2_Hayao', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:8}). Each style is fetched THROUGH Cache Storage (cache name animegan-onnx-cache) under its own repo path so lib/model-cache.js scans '/vumichien/AnimeGANv2_Hayao/' and a returning visit auto-initialises Hayao with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~8 MB). Verified in headless Chrome: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it downloaded + flipped the loader to ready. A per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.
rubric.notes: The loader tracks the default style (Hayao) as the representative; switching to Shinkai/Paprika lazily downloads ~8 MB with an on-page status. Small models keep the cost low.

rubric.evidence: Loading/progress/ready/error states are announced in role=status aria-live regions (shared loader + page #status). The Anime-fy status discloses when a first-use style downloads ~8 MB. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Stylization failed: …'); the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path. No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere (conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds).
rubric.notes: Measured 0 console errors and 0 network failures on load AND run across all 5 pages.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the 3 style chips (role=group, aria-pressed toggle, ≥2.4rem tap height), the accessible before/after reveal range (aria-valuetext '% anime'), the sample gallery (rights-safe thumbnails as labelled buttons), the upload/drop dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space, drag+drop), the Anime-fy button, and a Download-anime anchor. Ladder pages add: wild's three-style style-off; multi-model's MODNet matte + backdrop select.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built image-to-image demos; the reveal wipe uses a native range input for full keyboard support.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real AnimeGANv2 ONNX graph and the displayed image + restyle-amount + downloaded PNG are all derived from the real tanh output tensor. There is NO synthetic pre-degradation (unlike deblur/dehaze/denoise which add a known degradation); this is a straight photo->anime conversion, so what you see is exactly the model's output.
rubric.notes: Honest by construction. A rendered-output screenshot was reviewed to confirm the style is a genuine repaint.

rubric.evidence: All canvases carry role=img + descriptive aria-labels (source photo / anime repaint / per-style outputs / sticker); every button has an accessible name (conformance buttons-have-names pass); the loader status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext; the style chips are a labelled role=group with aria-pressed. Light+dark inherit the WCAG-AA design system. Gallery thumbnails are real buttons with aria-pressed + license attribution on selection.
rubric.notes: Consulted modern-web-guidance for accessible live-region status, toggle-button semantics, and exposing canvas content; applied.

rubric.evidence: Mobile+desktop MATRIX measured 0 px horizontal overflow on ALL 5 pages at mobile (360×740, DPR3) AND desktop (1280×800). The two-col layout is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; the param-grid is auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); the style-row flex-wraps; reveal/canvases are inline-size:100%; the wild page's three-panel grid wraps. scripts/responsive-check on the index reported desktop clean · mobile clean.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok. Mobile download cost (~8 MB/style fp32) surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the dense tanh->RGBA composite run in the Web Worker; the styled ImageBitmap is transferred, not cloned (invariant 15's dense-output-off-main-thread rule). The main thread only draws bounded fit-canvases + the reveal composite. A pass is ~1.3 s @256 to ~4 s @512 on single-thread WASM; the UI never blocks — INP stays low.
rubric.notes: Default maxSide 512 for the primary run, 384 for the multi-panel wild/multi-model pages to keep them snappy.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy image inference off main thread web worker; dense output composite without blocking
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks; break up / offload heavy synchronous processing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference AND the tanh->RGBA composite run in a dedicated module Web Worker; the styled ImageBitmap is transferred back (zero-copy). The main thread does only bounded canvas draws.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([styledBmp]); UI responsive during stylization; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel with style toggle chips and before/after reveal without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing, logical properties and flexible tracks (fr/minmax) before fixed widths; content-first flexbox with flex-wrap; use min-inline-size:0 so flex children can shrink — fewer media queries, no overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — two-col is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; param-grid is grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); the style-row flex-wraps; reveal/canvases inline-size:100% block-size:auto; logical properties throughout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px horizontal overflow measured on all 5 pages at mobile 360×740 DPR3 AND desktop 1280×800

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible toggle button group (style selector) and live region status; expose canvas-rendered content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use a labelled role=group with aria-pressed toggle buttons; announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the style chips are a role=group (aria-labelledby) of aria-pressed buttons; shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; canvases carry role=img + aria-label; the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; style chips toggle aria-pressed; canvas aria-labels present

openQuestions: AnimeGAN also ships a face-portrait checkpoint (akhaliq/AnimeGANv2-ONNX, face_paint_512) tuned for selfies; adding it as a fourth style would improve portrait results.
The models are fp32 (~8 MB each); a quantised export would shave the per-style download, though 8 MB is already light.

followUpGoals.goal: Add the face-portrait AnimeGANv2 checkpoint (face_paint_512) as an additional style tuned for selfies/portraits — additive, same page.
Add an AnimeGANv3 demo (portrait-sketch / JP-face styles) as a NEW page once its ONNX I/O is verified runnable — a distinct newer stylizer alongside v2.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for animegan-cartoonization (vumichien/AnimeGANv2_{Hayao,Shinkai,Paprika}, image-to-image cartoonization / photo->anime, fp32 ~8 MB/style — the showcase's FIRST photo->art stylization). Built as the wave's cartoonization target; the requested deraining target has no browser-runnable ONNX and was recorded status:blocked (all MAXIM/PReNet/Restormer-derain checkpoints are JAX/.npz or .pth; JPEG-artifact/retinex/HDR substitutes are also .ckpt/.pth-only). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED FIRST in headless Chrome: the real ONNX graphs repainted photos as anime (NHWC [-1,1] I/O, dynamic size), normalized RMSE(out,in) 0.10-0.17, and a rendered-output screenshot confirmed a genuine Shinkai-style repaint (not a colour filter). transformers.js has no AnimeGAN class, so it runs the ONNX directly via a per-worker onnxruntime-web pin. Full demo driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver: Download ~8 MB -> auto-init ready -> real output on the index + all 4 ladder pages (basics/practical restyle + PNG avatar download; wild three-style style-off; multi-model chained MODNet matting for an anime cut-out sticker). responsive-check 0 px overflow on all 5 pages at desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Ladder: basics (anime-fy), practical (anime avatar + download), wild (three-animator style-off), multi-model (anime -> matte -> sticker). NOTE: building the multi-model surfaced and fixed a pre-existing ReferenceError ('buf is not defined') in the shared modnet + rmbg matting workers (a separate committed bugfix). License Apache-2.0.
